commit.message:
Fix PR1 review + App Store prep + auto build numbers (#2)

* Address PR1 review + App Store prep + build number + screenshot infra

PR1 review comments (all from Copilot Code Review):

1. setup-repo.sh: remove dead PROTECTED_BRANCHES array. REQUIRED_CHECKS
   is now the single source of truth for which branches get protected.
2. setup-repo.sh: add `command -v jq` preflight check so the script
   fails with an actionable error instead of a confusing one.
3. promote.sh: stop parsing gh pr create output via `2>&1 | tail -1`
   (brittle). Let gh print the URL directly and fail if empty.
4. FeedbackView.swift: rewrite the security comment to be honest about
   the token shipping inside the app bundle. Explain the fine-grained
   PAT tradeoff and point to the webhook-only / proxy-server paths for
   production builds that can't tolerate credential exposure.
5. ci_post_clone.sh: gate token injection on a new
   HOUSEHEROES_EMBED_TOKEN_IN_RELEASE env var. Release-like workflows
   (CI_WORKFLOW contains "Release" / CI_BRANCH = release/appstore) no
   longer embed the github_token unless you explicitly opt in. Webhook
   URL is still used if set.
6. Secrets.json.template: fix misleading "never leaves your machine"
   wording. The file is bundled into the app — its contents ship with
   the binary.
7. release-tag.yml: drop --latest from auto-generated date tag release
   creation so manual semver releases (v1.0.0 etc.) stay pinned as
   latest on the repo homepage.

Safari fallback removed:
- FeedbackView.submit() no longer has an openGitHubInSafari() fallback.
  If neither github_token nor webhook_url is configured, show a clear
  error in the UI instead of silently bouncing the user to Safari.
- The "View it" button on the success alert now copies the issue URL
  to clipboard instead of opening Safari.
- openedInSafari / openedInMail SendResult cases removed along with
  the now-dead openGitHubInSafari function.
- Debug logging on Secrets.json load so we can tell at a glance
  whether the token is being read correctly.

Build number auto-increment:
- ci_scripts/ci_pre_xcodebuild.sh: stamps CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION from
  CI_BUILD_NUMBER via sed before xcodebuild runs.
- ci_scripts/ci_post_xcodebuild.sh: minimal, silences the
  "post-Xcodebuild script not found" warning in Xcode Cloud logs and
  is a clean hook for future post-build work.

Auto-address Copilot review comments:
- New workflow .github/workflows/copilot-review-followup.yml that
  triggers on pull_request_review / pull_request_review_comment from
  Copilot and re-assigns the PR to Copilot Coding Agent to apply the
  suggested fixes. No merge happens — Sjoerd still approves.

App Store submission prep:
- HouseHeroes/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy: required Apple privacy manifest
  declaring no tracking, Other User Content as the only collected data
  type, and Required Reason API usage for UserDefaults, FileTimestamp,
  DiskSpace, and SystemBootTime.
- PRIVACY_POLICY.md: hostable privacy policy template covering Apple
  iCloud, GitHub feedback, catbox.moe, and user rights.
- HouseHeroesApp.swift: --screenshot-mode launch argument that seeds
  an in-memory store with demo data via ScreenshotSeed.
- HouseHeroes/Persistence/ScreenshotSeed.swift: aspirational household
  with 2 members, 7 chores, 40 days of dishes history (mastery crown),
  4 badges, and real-looking streak/point counts.
- scripts/screenshots/capture.sh: boots iPhone 16 Pro Max simulator,
  builds + installs + launches with the flag, prompts you to navigate
  each tab, captures at native 1320x2868 for App Store Connect.

Skill sync:
- ~/.claude/skills/ios-app-bootstrap/ updated with the new PrivacyInfo
  template, screenshot capture script, ScreenshotSeed template, and
  all updated workflows + scripts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address all 7 Copilot review comments from PR2

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Sjoerd-Bo3/househeroes/sessions/d72b76cd-735b-468a-bb97-0d4927d8bafc

Co-authored-by: Sjoerd-Bo3 <2100083+Sjoerd-Bo3@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR2 review + add screenshot auto-capture + auto-resolve threads

PR2 review comments (all legitimate, all from Copilot Code Review):

- promote.sh: the comment claimed --json url --jq .url but the command
  didn't pass those flags. Fixed by calling 'gh pr create' without URL
  capture, then 'gh pr view --json url --jq .url' to re-query cleanly.
- capture.sh: APP_PATH was found via head -1 on DerivedData which can
  pick up a stale build from a different checkout. Now uses
  'xcodebuild -showBuildSettings' to get BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR directly.
- ScreenshotSeed.swift: raw recurrence strings ("daily", "weekly") and
  raw status strings ("completed", "pending") replaced with
  Frequency.*.rawValue and ChoreStatus.*.rawValue so the seed stays in
  sync if either enum is renamed.
- ScreenshotSeed.swift: try? ctx.save() replaced with do/catch that
  prints on failure — screenshot mode is completely broken if the seed
  fails to persist, and silent failures make that hard to debug.
- FeedbackView.swift: the "not set up" error message hardcoded the
  email address instead of using FeedbackConfig.contactEmail. Fixed.

Screenshot automation:

- HouseHeroesApp.swift: new --screen launch arg (today/chores/crew/
  stats/settings) that writes the requested tab index to UserDefaults
  via 'screenshotStartTab' so RootView's MainTabView picks it up for
  initial selection. Screenshot mode also skips RecurrenceEngine +
  NotificationService side effects so the seeded state stays stable
  for capture.
- RootView.swift: MainTabView reads 'screenshotStartTab' at init to
  honor the launch arg.
- capture.sh: fully rewrites — no more manual tapping. For each tab,
  terminates any running instance, launches with
  '--screenshot-mode --screen <tab>', waits for render, captures via
  'simctl io screenshot' to screenshots/6.9/.

Auto-address + auto-resolve Copilot review flow:

- Delete the ineffective copilot-review-followup.yml (it tried to
  re-assign Coding Agent to address review comments, but Coding Agent
  only operates on issues — it ignored the PR assignment).
- New request-copilot-fix.yml: when Copilot Code Review submits a
  review, posts "@copilot please address the review comments" as a PR
  comment. Copilot Coding Agent picks up the mention and pushes fixes
  directly to the PR branch.
- New resolve-threads-after-copilot.yml: when Copilot's bot pushes
  commits to a PR branch (pull_request synchronize event with sender
  == Copilot bot), queries all unresolved review threads via GraphQL
  and marks each one resolved via the resolveReviewThread mutation.
  Closes the "Copilot fixed the code but threads are still unresolved"
  gap that was blocking merges.
- New release-branch-assign.yml: on any PR opened against
  release/testflight or release/appstore, auto-assigns Sjoerd-Bo3 as
  the assignee so release PRs show up in his dashboard even when he's
  the PR author (CODEOWNERS auto-request is skipped for self-authored
  PRs).

Branch protection relaxed on main:

- setup-repo.sh now takes a third field in REQUIRED_CHECKS
  (approval_count), which defaults to 0 for main and 1 for release
  branches. For a solo dev, self-approving a PR into main was friction
  without value — the PR requirement, conversation resolution, status
  checks, and linear history rules are all still enforced.
- Release branches still require the approval step as a last-chance
  sanity check before code hits TestFlight / App Store.

Python: Stop emitting duplicate reasoning content from OpenAI `response.reasoning_text.done` and `response.reasoning_summary_text.done` events (#5162)

* Fix reasoning text done events duplicating streamed delta content (#5157)

The OpenAI Responses API sends both reasoning_text.delta (incremental
chunks) and reasoning_text.done (full accumulated text) events. The
chat client was emitting Content for both, causing ag-ui to append the
full done text onto already-accumulated delta text, producing
duplicated reasoning output.

Stop emitting Content for reasoning_text.done and
reasoning_summary_text.done events, matching how output_text.done is
already handled (not emitted). The deltas contain all the content;
the done event is redundant.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(openai): emit reasoning done content as fallback when no deltas observed (#5157)

Address PR review feedback:
- Track item_ids that received reasoning deltas via seen_reasoning_delta_item_ids set
- Emit content from done events only when no deltas were received for the
  item_id, preventing silent content loss on stream resumption
- Add comment documenting code_interpreter done event asymmetry
- Replace redundant ag-ui test with deduplication-focused test
- Add integration test for delta+done sequence in OpenAI chat client tests
- Add fallback path tests for done events without preceding deltas

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback for #5157: Python: [Bug]: "type": "response.reasoning_text.delta" and "response.reasoning_text.done" both get exposed as "text_reasoning"

* Fix AG-UI reasoning streaming to use proper Start/End pattern (#5157)

_emit_text_reasoning now follows the same streaming pattern as _emit_text:
- Emits ReasoningStartEvent/ReasoningMessageStartEvent only on the first
  delta for a given message_id
- Emits only ReasoningMessageContentEvent for subsequent deltas
- Defers ReasoningMessageEndEvent/ReasoningEndEvent until
  _close_reasoning_block is called (on content type switch or end-of-run)

This produces the correct protocol pattern:
  ReasoningStartEvent
    ReasoningMessageStartEvent
    ReasoningMessageContentEvent(delta1)
    ReasoningMessageContentEvent(delta2)
    ReasoningMessageEndEvent
  ReasoningEndEvent

Instead of wrapping every delta in a full Start→End sequence.

Backward compatibility is preserved: calling _emit_text_reasoning without
a flow argument still produces the full sequence per call.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix import ordering lint error in AG-UI test file (#5157)

Move inline import of TextMessageContentEvent to the top-level import
block and ensure alphabetical ordering to satisfy ruff I001 rule.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: AWS credential fallback robust against AWS_PROFILE set to missing profile (#1104)

* fix: AWS credential fallback should not require default profile in config

When a specified AWS profile is not found, the fallback was using
WithSharedConfigProfile("default") which still requires a [default]
section in ~/.aws/config. EC2 users with no config file at all would
panic with "failed to get shared config profile, default".

Remove the explicit profile override in the fallback so the SDK uses
its default credential chain (env vars, shared credentials, EC2 IMDS)
without requiring any profile to exist in config files.

Fixes #1094

Signed-off-by: yxxhero <aiopsclub@163.com>

* fix: handle AWS_PROFILE env var in profile-not-found fallback path

commit.message:
Fix wrist quaternion: use exact parent rotation, not approximation

The previous computeWristQuat used setFromUnitVectors((0,1,0), forearmDir)
to approximate the parent rotation. This gives the correct Y-axis mapping
but WRONG twist around the forearm axis, causing the hand segment to
point in the wrong direction (paddle behind wrist instead of in front).

Fix: compute the exact parent rotation analytically as
R_y(-phi1) * R_x(phi2+phi3-pi/2), matching the actual Three.js hierarchy
(shoulderYaw * shoulderPitch * elbow). Then transform the desired paddle
normal to wrist-local space and use setFromUnitVectors to get the wrist
quaternion.

FK verification: 0.0mm position error AND exact normal match for all
test positions and tilt values.

Merge open PR fixes into main-ready branch

* security: fix insecure temporary file and directory creation

Addresses a security vulnerability where deterministic temporary file and lock
file names could be manipulated via symlink attacks in potentially shared
temporary directories.

This fix implements secure, per-task work directories in both the Bash and
Python network utility modules. These directories are created with restricted
permissions (700) and are explicitly verified to be real directories owned
by the current user before use, effectively mitigating symlink and race
condition attacks.

Changes:
- scripts/lib/network.sh: Implement per-task work_dir with ownership and
  symlink verification. Update cookie_file to be task-specific.
- scripts/utils/network.py: Implement _get_secure_work_dir with mode,
  ownership, and symlink verification. Update download functions to use it.
- tests/test_network.py: Update tests to match the new secure directory
  structure.
- Fix a PermissionError in Python tests by handling chmod failures in
  restricted environments.

* fix

* fix: handle int64 inverse/counts in unique_consecutive for nightly PyTorch

In PyTorch nightly, aten.unique_consecutive.default reports int64 for
inverse-indices and counts outputs in the FX graph even when input is
int32. The ONNX converter was using itype (input type = int32) for these
outputs, causing an AssertionError in _set_shape_and_type when it tried
to set the already-recorded int32 type to int64.

Fix: read per-output dtypes from sts['dtype'] (a tuple for multi-output
ops) and use inv_itype/cnt_itype for building inverse/counts ONNX ops.

Test update: test_aten_unique_consecutive_return_32 now casts expected
values to match ONNX output types, making it work for both old torch
(FX reports int32) and nightly (FX reports int64).

- EXP-1681: Over-rescue dominates (53%), no minimal-rescue observed
- EXP-1682: Can't predict rebound at nadir (AUC=0.54)
- EXP-1683: Demand vacuum effect weak (r=-0.13)
- EXP-1684: Overnight hypos worst (69% → hyperglycemia)
- EXP-1685: Therapy settings don't predict hypo rate (n=11)
- EXP-1686: S×D equilibrium restored in 1.7h median
- EXP-1687: ALL patients show clustered hypos (26% cascade in 6h)
- EXP-1688: Hyper-rebound rate predicts TAR (r=0.791, p=0.004)

Key findings:
- 55% of cascading hypos follow hyper-rebound (hypo→hyper→hypo cycle)
- Post-hypo management drives overall glycemic control more than hypo
  frequency itself
- Patient k: 1.2 hypo/day, 0% hyper, 84.7% TIR = gold standard rescue

Renumbered from 1651 to 1681 to avoid collision with parallel research.

commit.message:
Add mappers 135, 136, 137, 138, 139 (Sachen 8259 family + Sachen 3011) (#1930)

Implements five Sachen-board mappers:

- Mapper 135: Sachen 8259A (2 KiB CHR banks, shift=1, OR=[1,0,1])
- Mapper 136: Sachen 3011 / JV001 ASIC (6-bit latch/increment/invert chip)
- Mapper 137: Sachen 8259D (1 KiB CHR banks, reversed mirroring polarity)
- Mapper 138: Sachen 8259B (2 KiB CHR banks, no shift/OR)
- Mapper 139: Sachen 8259C (2 KiB CHR banks, shift=2, OR=[1,2,3])

Mappers 135/137/138/139 share a single Sachen8259 struct that selects
the variant from the mapper number at construction time. All four use
a mapper-internal 2 KiB CIRAM with per-quadrant nametable routing
controlled by register 7 (simple mode + 2-bit mirroring select).

Mapper 136 is a standalone struct implementing the JV001 ASIC copy-
protection logic (latch/increment/invert on a 6-bit register, output
applied to banks on 000 write).

Unit tests are included for all five mappers covering PRG/CHR banking,
nametable mirroring modes, reset, and snapshot/restore.

Closes #1029
Closes #1031
Closes #1033
Closes #1035
Closes #1037

commit.message:
perf(interp): optimize closure call path for closure_counter_1k benchmark

Three targeted optimizations for the closure call hot path:

1. Raise INLINE_BYTECODE_THRESHOLD from 40 to 120 so more real-world
   closures (counter callbacks, event handlers) take the fast inline
   path and avoid frame allocation + register pool overhead.

2. Add Smi fast path in inline_context_slot_update and
   inline_chained_context_slot_update for AddSmi/SubSmi opcodes.
   Previously, even Smi values went through to_number() -> f64 ->
   number_to_jsvalue() round-trip. Now Smi + Smi stays in integer
   arithmetic with checked_add/checked_sub, only falling back to
   f64 on overflow.

3. Specialize CallUndefinedReceiver0 dispatch: when a closure already
   has JIT code (Maglev/Turbofan), try JIT execution immediately
   before the inline pattern matching and tiering logic. This avoids
   redundant bytecode decoding and invocation count bookkeeping for
   hot closures that have already been JIT-compiled.
